data.title: High density polyethylene (HDPE) export prices journey northward in the United States
data.description: This week, HDPE export prices witnessed a rise in the US.An industry source in the U.S. informed a Polymerupdate team member, "US spot export prices for high-density polyethylene (HDPE) remained stable or experienced slight increases across most grades during the week ended, as market sentiment began to shift towards the perception that prices may have reached a minimum threshold. Producers maintained their current pricing levels and resisted further reductions, prompting market participants to adjust their margins to stay competitive. Additionally, cautious optimism was bolstered by the announcement of a temporary reduction in trade tensions between the US and China. The two nations agreed to a 90-day tariff reduction starting May 14, with the US lowering tariffs on most Chinese goods from 145% to 30%, while China reduced tariffs on US goods from 125% to 10%. This development raised expectations for a gradual normalization of trade flows, with China once again viewed as a viable market for US-origin materials. Market feedback indicated that previously available low-priced offers have mostly been absorbed, further supporting the idea that prices may stabilize or begin to rise. However, a level of uncertainty remained, as the tariff relief is temporary and contingent upon the results of broader trade negotiations.HDPE blow moulding export prices at USD 820-840/mt FAS Houston levels, a surge of USD (+45/mt) from last week. HDPE film export prices were assessed at USD 840-860/mt FAS Houston levels, up USD (+10/mt) from the previous week.Meanwhile, HDPE injection prices were assessed at the USD 820-840/mt FAS Houston levels, steady from last week.Spot upstream ethylene prices on Thursday were assessed at the 19.00-19.50 cents/lb FOB U.S. Gulf levels, a marginal week on week drop of (-0.50 cent/lb).The US ethylene contract price for April 2025 settled at the 31.50 cents/lb mark. This price represents a decline of 3.75 cents/lb from its March 2025 settlement levels."

data.title: Croatia’s annual inflation stagnates at 3.2% in Apr
data.description:
ZAGREB (Croatia), May 16 (SeeNews) - The prices of goods and services in Croatia rose by 3.2% on the year in April, after increasing at the same pace in March, the statistical office said on Friday, revising its initial estimate for a 3.1% inflation in April.

On a monthly comparison basis, Croatia's consumer price index (CPI) increased 0.6% in April, after advancing 0.4% in March, the Bureau of Statistics said in a press release.

In April, restaurant and hotel service prices saw the highest annual increase, of 8.7%, followed by recreation and culture with a rise of 5.9%.

Measured by the EU-harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP), prices of goods and services for personal consumption in Croatia increased by an annual 4% in April, after adding 4.3% on average in March.

On a monthly comparison basis, HICP grew 0.7% in April, after it rose 0.4% in March.

In 2024, Croatia's average annual inflation eased to 3.0% from to 8.0% in 2023. EU-harmonised prices rose by an average 4.0% last year, compared to 8.4% in 2023.

data.title: Paraxylene (PX) prices dip in the United States
data.description: PX prices down adjusted in the US on Thursday.An industry source in the US on condition of anonymity informed a Polymerupdate team member, "PX prices in the US have decreased in line with falling paraxylene market values in Asia. PX prices were assessed at the USD 905-915/mt FOB US Gulf mark, a fall of USD (-15/mt) from Wednesday's assessed levels.Upstream MX prices were assessed at the 276.00-276.10 cents/gallon FOB US Gulf levels, a day on day decrease of (-3 cents/gallon).Meanwhile in Asia, PX prices on Thursday were assessed at the USD 830-840/mt FOB Korea levels, down USD (-15/mt) from Wednesday.
